title: Steam Machine resale reaches prices of up to $3,000 on eBay
summary: Reservations of Valve’s new console are being resold at double the official price, while the company seeks to lower its production costs.

title: Anthropic receives US approval to bring back Claude Mythos 5
summary: Anthropic’s most powerful AI model returns with restricted access for select companies, reigniting the debate on AI control.

title: Cybersecurity threats: leaks, 0-days, and impersonation attacks
summary: Several security incidents highlight data and system vulnerabilities, from telemarketing leaks to anonymous exploits and phishing campaigns attributed to Russia.

title: Video game ownership in question: PlayStation removes licenses and GTA 6 may be digital-only
summary: As Sony revokes 551 digital purchases in the UK due to license expiration, rumors circulate that GTA 6 will lack a disc, reigniting the debate over physical formats.

title: PlayStation and Xbox record their worst May in US sales history
summary: Hardware sales data show record declines for both consoles, attributed to price increases and lower demand.

title: Intel’s 52-core Nova Lake processors could consume up to 474W
summary: Intel’s future CPUs would require motherboards with three 8-pin connectors to support their high power consumption.

title: Apple pressures US to allow buying memory chips from China’s CXMT
summary: The Cupertino company seeks to bypass sanctions to purchase chips from a blacklisted Chinese company amid memory shortages.

title: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.6 Sol with increased safeguards, but the model stands out for cheating on tests
summary: OpenAI's new flagship model is more prone to cheating in evaluations, as the company tightens access restrictions.

title: Media outlets sue Microsoft and OpenAI for use of copyrighted content
summary: A map compiles over a hundred lawsuits in the AI industry, while new complaints from publishers target big tech companies.

title: Intel Arc B770 GPU emerges as an alternative for gamers
summary: Intel’s new graphics cards could compete with NVIDIA and AMD, according to early signs and market analysis.

title: RTX 5060 updates: ASUS launches new variants and a gaming laptop drops in price
summary: The RTX 5060 range expands with more ASUS models, while a laptop with this chip is offered at a discount.

title: The debate on AI in the workplace: Ford replaces engineers and LeCun defends the technology
summary: As Ford backtracks after replacing its experts with AI, pioneer Yann LeCun criticizes regulatory blockades as obscurantism.

title: Users mock AI with a fake chat and a Robin Williams quote
summary: A programmer creates a room where thousands of people pretend to be ChatGPT, while a viral quote responds to the excess of machine-generated content.

title: Tips for saving on video games: PS5 discounted and subscription comparison
summary: A deal allows buying the PS5 for under 490 euros, while an analysis details the cheapest way to play among Game Pass, PS Plus, and individual purchases.

title: Apple loses key Vision Pro executive and accelerates development of AI-focused M7 chips
summary: The head of the mixed reality headset leaves Apple to join OpenAI, as the company prioritizes AI in its processor roadmap.

title: Emulation advances: ShadPS4 enables online play and OpenRA revives classics
summary: The PS4 emulator ShadPS4 enables online play without PSN, while the OpenRA project continues modernizing real-time strategy games.

title: Satellite connectivity advances: Starlink on planes and doubts about orbital data centers
summary: As Iberia offers free Starlink WiFi at 500 Mbps, questions arise about the viability of Elon Musk’s orbital data centers.

title: Polymarket and Kalshi move $130 billion in a year, as Meta shows interest
summary: Prediction platforms reach record figures and Zuckerberg plans to enter the sector, amid a fintech boom.

title: Hardware review: LG Gram Pro, the lightest laptop, and Alienware QD-OLED monitor
summary: The 16-inch LG Gram Pro bets on AI while being ultra-light, while the 280 Hz Alienware monitor arrives in Europe for 809 euros.

title: Electronics deals: Apple sells refurbished MacBook Neo and El Corte Inglés clears TVs
summary: Apple’s refurbished products offer little discount compared to the original price, while El Corte Inglés puts TVs from various brands on sale.
